codeReview.summary: Implementation fully covers the feature requirements. Created business_type table with 5 business types (beauty, health, fitness, professional, technical), added business_type_id foreign key to profile table, created seed data with Spanish names, and implemented API endpoints to retrieve business types. The build was verified successfully.

issues.description: The /:id endpoint doesn't validate UUID format before querying. Invalid UUIDs will cause an unhandled error instead of returning a proper 404 response. Consider adding UUID validation or error handling.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker did not create a repository/business service layer for business_type - direct db queries are used in the route handler. This appears acceptable for a simple global configuration table (read-only) and matches patterns used by similar endpoints like payment-methods and inventory in the codebase.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: packages/api/src/api/routes/business-types.ts:4 - uses db.query directly without service layer

sharedStateObservations.observation: The system prompt referenced .factory/services.yaml but this file doesn't exist in the project. This may indicate incomplete setup or documentation drift.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Glob search for services.yaml in project returned no results

summary: Feature implementation passes review. All expected behaviors were implemented correctly: business_type table with schema fields (id, name, key, description, icon, isActive), seed data for 5 business types with Spanish names, profile.business_type_id foreign key with ON DELETE SET NULL, and API endpoints GET /api/business-types and GET /api/business-types/:id. The build verified successfully. One minor non-blocking issue noted: UUID validation missing in the /:id endpoint.
